name: Manila (Philippines)

colors.symbolism: the field — environmental renewal and the city's aspirations
the gold of the city seal
the white ring of the seal

description: A green field charged at the centre with the gold seal of the City of Manila — a shield with a gold sea-lion above waves and a pearl in a seashell, encircled by a white band bearing the city's name and six gold stars.
history: Manila, the capital of the Philippines, uses its civic seal — designed by Galo B. Ocampo and adopted in 1972 — as its primary emblem. The modern municipal flag places the seal on a green field.

features.symbolism: a green field as background for the city's seal

features.role: Seal of the City of Manila
features.symbolism: The city seal — a shield with a gold sea-lion above stylised waves of Manila Bay and a pearl in a seashell, encircled by the Latin/Filipino inscription and six gold stars for the city's six congressional districts.
